description_long: This house is a single-story structure elevated on stilts, featuring a rectangular footprint and a simple, functional design. The roof is a prominent gable style, extending over the front porch area, which is accessed by a set of steps and protected by a railing. The exterior walls are light-colored, with several rectangular windows arranged in a linear fashion along the sides, providing natural light to the interior. The front entryway is defined by a covered porch with vertical supports and a small set of stairs leading up from the ground. A long, horizontal fence or barrier runs parallel to one side of the house, possibly serving as a property boundary or privacy screen. The house sits on a flat, grassy terrain, suggesting a suburban or semi-rural setting.
description: A single-story, stilted house with a gable roof, covered front porch, and linear window arrangement, set on grassy terrain with a side fence.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, entry steps, and railing, with the gable roof overhead.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the linear arrangement of windows and the long fence running parallel to the house.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back wall, roofline, and the full length of the side fence.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the stilt supports, windows, and the open space beneath the house.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with steps, railing, and vertical supports beneath a gable roof overhang
